chapter_title.en: Chapter 94 – The Salāt regarding seeking of the livelihood

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/458)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/458)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَا وَلِيدُ أَيْنَ حَانُوتُكَ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ فَقُلْتُ عَلَى بَابِهِ فَقَالَ إِذَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تَأْتِيَ حَانُوتَكَ فَابْدَأْ بِالْمَسْجِدِ فَصَلِّ فِيهِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ أَوْ أَرْبَعاً ثُمَّ قُلْ غَدَوْتُ بِحَوْلِ اللَّهِ وَ قُوَّتِهِ وَ غَدَوْتُ بِلَا حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَ لَا قُوَّةٍ بَلْ بِحَوْلِكَ وَ قُوَّتِكَ يَا رَبِّ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي عَبْدُكَ أَلْتَمِسُ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ كَمَا أَمَرْتَنِي فَيَسِّرْ لِي ذَلِكَ وَ أَنَا خَافِضٌ فِي عَافِيَتِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al-Hakam, from Ibn Al-Waleed Bin Sabeeh, from his father who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘O Waleed! Where is your shop from the Masjid?’ So I said, ‘By its door’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whenever you intend to go to your shop, so begin with the Masjid, and pray two Rak’at therein, or four, then say, ‘I came with the Might of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Strength, and I came without any might from me nor strength, but, by You<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Your<sup>azwj</sup> Strength, O Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the worlds. I am Your<sup>azwj</sup> servant seeking from Your<sup>azwj</sup> Grace just as You<sup>azwj</sup> Commanded me, therefore Make that to be easy for me, and I am free from anxiety in Your<sup>azwj</sup> well-being’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Afi ibn af-Hakam from ibn af-Wafid ibn Sabih from his father who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, asked me, ‘Where is your shop in relation to the Masjid?’ I replied, ‘It is near the door of the Masjid.’ He then said, ‘When you want to go to your shop, first go to the Masjid, perform two or four Rak‘at Salah (prayer) and at the end say, “I have attended this morning by the means and power of Allah, but not by my own means and power, in fact it is by Your means and power O Lord. O Allah, I am Your servant and I appeal to You to grant me (wealth) through Your generosity, as You have commanded me to pray to You and appeal. Make this possible for me with ease, so I can be affluent and in good health by Your help.’””
